Not contested and tentative ruling adopted.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party David Beck

RULING:
DEFENDANT TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE ISSUES

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Todd Shipyards Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. As to the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Hixson's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Beck was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. As to defendant's argument based on Privette v. Superior Court and its progeny, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that even if plaintiff cannot demonstrate that an exception to Privette applies, plaintiff's claim still fails. Plaintiff represents that he did not allege causes of action for strict liability or false representation against defendant and those were the only causes of action defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
